policy.rule: Refactor-resilience tests must protect externally observable contracts, not internal function names or implementation layout.
policy.bug_fossilization_guard: Golden expectations must classify each behavior as intended, documented-limitation, or known-bug-blocker. A known bug must not become a runnable passing invariant.

cases.observable_contract: Docker-shaped /_ping, /version, /info, images, containers, networks, volumes, and events responses remain stable across backend refactors.
cases.invariant: Response status classes, required keys, and compatibility warnings remain Docker-compatible or explicitly documented.
cases.refactor_risk: Route splitting, daemon state reshaping, or metadata-store replacement can silently change response shapes.

cases.command: python3 scripts/verify-project-library.py && python3 scripts/verify-dockerfile-standard.py && python3 -m unittest tests.test_dockerfile_copy_compat
cases.observable_contract: Bundled Compose and Dockerfile templates remain portable standard project definitions, and pdockerd's builder executes the standard Dockerfile constructs those templates use.
cases.invariant: No pdocker-only Dockerfile instructions, missing project files, invalid declared project routes, broken COPY wildcard expansion, or context-escape copies.
cases.refactor_risk: Builder-parser or copy-logic refactors can silently reject valid Dockerfile constructs while UI menus and static template syntax checks still pass.

cases.observable_contract: Expected abnormal paths keep stable triggers, expected signals, failure oracles, reproduction commands, and artifact hashes.
cases.invariant: Runnable abnormal cases have existing evidence artifacts and planned gaps remain explicit.
cases.refactor_risk: Error handling refactors can turn deterministic failures into unstructured console text or fake success.

cases.observable_contract: Image, container, job, daemon operation, UI card, and docker ps state transitions remain equivalent across internal state-store refactors.
cases.invariant: A container cannot be Up in cards and Exited in docker ps for the same container id; job completion and process state must converge.
cases.refactor_risk: Project-name keyed state, duplicate caches, or UI summaries can drift from canonical container ids.
cases.gap_reason: A canonical state-machine model and golden transition corpus are not implemented yet.

cases.observable_contract: Archive copy-in/copy-out preserves tar headers, file modes, symlinks, whiteouts, and merged lower/upper views across storage refactors within the documented Docker compatibility scope.
cases.invariant: Tar round-trip produces the same externally visible file tree and metadata within the declared pdocker compatibility scope.
cases.refactor_risk: COW or metadata-index refactors can pass simple file tests while breaking Docker archive interoperability.
cases.gap_reason: Host archive API compatibility is runnable, but full archive round-trip device promotion still depends on storage kill-at-step and broader differential fixtures.

cases.observable_contract: Refactors compare current JSON artifacts against a previous accepted baseline and classify allowed drift versus blockers.
cases.invariant: Schema, key presence, status vectors, failure classes, and benchmark envelopes cannot drift without an explicit recorded reason.
cases.refactor_risk: Large refactors can regenerate valid artifacts with changed meaning while individual verifiers still pass.
cases.gap_reason: Accepted-baseline storage and drift-classification rules are not implemented yet.
